title: Welcome to Your Vault
content:
---
tags: home, welcome
---

# Welcome to Your Vault 👋

Take a deep breath and relax. This is your personal space to think, write, and connect your ideas. 

Whether you're writing code, drafting a novel, or organizing your thoughts, we've got you covered.

## Where to start?
If you're new here, we recommend checking out the [[/Formatting Guide/Basic Typography]] guide, or jumping straight into [[/Formatting Guide/Advanced Components]] if you want to see what this editor can really do.

Curious about how your notes connect? Open the [[/Knowledge Base/The Graph View]].

title: Basic Typography
content:
# Basic Typography

Writing should feel natural. Just start typing, and use simple symbols to format your text.

## Emphasis
Make your words stand out with **bold** text, *italics*, or ~~strikethrough~~. You can also ==highlight important phrases== to easily find them later.

## Lists
Organize your thoughts into clear lists:

### Unordered
- First, find a quiet place.
- Second, open a new note.
  - *Pro tip: use shortcuts!*

### Ordered
1. Write down your ideas.
2. Connect them using `[[links]]`.
3. Review your [[/Knowledge Base/The Graph View]].

> "The palest ink is better than the best memory."
> — Chinese Proverb

Ready for more? Check out [[/Formatting Guide/Advanced Components]].

title: Advanced Components
content:
# Advanced Components

When plain text isn't enough, we give you the tools to structure complex data.

## Tables
Keep your data neat and tidy:

| Feature | Status | Difficulty |
| :--- | :---: | ---: |
| Markdown | Active | Easy |
| Graph View | Beta | Medium |
| Syncing | Planning | Hard |

## Code Snippets
Are you a developer? Use inline code like `npm install`, or create full code blocks:

```typescript
function initializeVault() {
  console.log('Vault loaded!');
}
```

See how to make these stand out even more in [[/Formatting Guide/Custom Callouts]].

title: Custom Callouts
content:
---
tags: callouts, ui
---

# Custom Callouts

Callouts are a fantastic way to draw attention to specific parts of your notes.

> [!info] Did you know?
> You can nest callouts, lists, and even other markdown elements inside here.

> [!success] Goal Achieved
> You've successfully learned how to format text!

> [!warning] Careful
> Deleting a note cannot be undone unless you have a backup.

## To-Do Lists
You can also track your progress right inside your notes:
- [x] Read [[/Welcome to Your Vault]]
- [x] Learn about [[/Formatting Guide/Basic Typography]]
- [ ] Master [[/Formatting Guide/Advanced Components]]
- [ ] Explore [[/Knowledge Base/The Graph View]]

title: The Graph View
content:
# The Graph View

This is where the magic happens. Every time you use double brackets to link to another note, like [[/Welcome to Your Vault]], a connection is formed.

## Why use links?
By linking [[/Formatting Guide/Basic Typography]], [[/Formatting Guide/Advanced Components]], and [[/Formatting Guide/Custom Callouts]] together, you create a web of knowledge.

Over time, clusters will form around your most important topics, such as #productivity or #development. This mimics how your brain actually works!

title: Example Content
content:
# Example Content

## Overview

This document demonstrates how markdown content is rendered in the editor, including support for code fences, syntax highlighting, and common formatting features.

It is intended as a visual showcase to verify how different markdown elements appear, rather than as formal documentation.

## What This Demo Covers

The examples below highlight:

- Code fences with syntax highlighting
- Inline code formatting
- Headings and text structure
- Lists and nesting
- Links and formatting
- Tables and extended markdown features

Use this page to test and preview how markdown is displayed in the system.

## Headings

```yaml
description: |
  # Heading 1
  ## Heading 2
  ### Heading 3
```

## Paragraphs and Line Breaks

```yaml
description: |
  This is a paragraph of text.

  This is another paragraph. Two newlines create a paragraph break.

  Use two spaces at the end of a line  
  to create a hard line break within a paragraph.
```

## Emphasis

```yaml
description: |
  *Italic text* or _italic text_

  **Bold text** or __bold text__

  ***Bold and italic*** or ___bold and italic___
```

## Lists

```yaml
description: |
  Unordered list:
  - Item one
  - Item two
    - Nested item
  - Item three

  Ordered list:
  1. First item
  2. Second item
  3. Third item
```

## Links

```yaml
description: |
  [Link text](https://example.com)

  [Link with title](https://example.com "Link title")

  Autolink: <https://example.com>
```

## Code

```yaml
description: |
  Inline `code` in text.

  Code block:
  ```json
  {
    "key": "value"
  }
  ```

  Indented code block:
      function example() {
        return true;
      }
```

## Blockquotes

```yaml
description: |
  > This is a blockquote.
  >
  > It can span multiple lines.

  > Nested blockquotes:
  > > Inner quote
```

## Horizontal Rules

```yaml
description: |
  Content above

  ---

  Content below
```

## Tables (GFM Extension)

```yaml
description: |
  | Column 1 | Column 2 | Column 3 |
  |----------|----------|----------|
  | Row 1    | Data     | Data     |
  | Row 2    | Data     | Data     |
```

## Task Lists (GFM Extension)

```yaml
description: |
  - [x] Completed task
  - [ ] Incomplete task
  - [ ] Another task
```

## Example Usage

```yaml
info:
  title: Demo API
  version: 1.0.0
  description: |
    # Demo API

    This section demonstrates a realistic markdown usage scenario.

    ## Features

    - **Example Data**
    - **Formatted Output**
    - **Syntax Highlighting**

    ## Example Request

    ```bash
    curl -X GET "https://api.example.com/data" \
      -H "Authorization: Bearer token"
    ```
```


## Best Practices

1. **Use headings for structure** — Break long descriptions into sections
2. **Include code examples** — Show request/response examples
3. **Use tables for lists of values** — Enums, status codes, parameters
4. **Add callouts for important info** — Use blockquotes for warnings/notes
5. **Link to external docs** — Reference detailed documentation
